hero.statement: Every perfect prison begins with a perfect equation.
hero.description: MATHBHOOT needs curious minds to create mathematical traps, characters, artifacts, rooms, and rules for an experiential horror-learning universe.
hero.imageAlt: A masked mathematical antagonist holding a glowing geometric construct
hero.orderText: Challenge protagonists through reasoning, patterns, technology, and real-life mathematics. Every idea must remain safe, original, lawful, and educational.

missions.title: Create a Mathematical Trap
missions.description: Design a safe challenge that uses real-life mathematics to confuse, test, and ultimately teach the protagonist.

missions.title: Build an NPC
missions.description: Create a student, teacher, ghost, guide, or historical mathematician with a meaningful role in MathVilla.

missions.title: Design an Ancient Artifact
missions.description: Invent a mathematical object whose powers and limitations follow understandable rules.

missions.title: Create a MathVilla Room
missions.description: Plan an atmospheric room where clues, technology, story, and mathematical reasoning work together.

missions.title: Design a New Rule
missions.description: Propose a mathematical law that controls part of MathVilla without contradicting established world logic.

application.heading: Become Part of the Shadow Network
application.description: Create a private draft on this device. Online accounts and submissions will open after the secure workspace is connected.

fields.label: What drives you to join MATHBHOOT?
fields.placeholder: Describe your curiosity, skills, and the kind of mathematical experiences you want to create.

application.consentLabel: I understand this is currently a voluntary creative program. Submission, approval, payment, employment, fame, or future revenue is not guaranteed.
application.draftNotice: This draft stays only in this browser. It is not sent to MATHBHOOT or any external service.

principles: Original work only; copied characters, stories, code, art, or puzzles are not accepted.
Horror must build atmosphere without encouraging real-world harm, harassment, discrimination, or dangerous acts.
Every trap must have a valid mathematical idea, an explainable solution, and an educational purpose.
Personal information, secrets, passwords, financial details, and identifying data must never be placed inside a concept draft.
Ownership, attribution, withdrawal, privacy, age eligibility, and contributor terms will be shown before online submission opens.

closing.linkLabel: Return to the Control Room Story
